free_text: Mass and charge distribution in Th-232(alpha,f)
reaction in the projectile energy range 28 to 72-MeV
Variable energy cyclotron.
Helium jet
facility.
Th-232 was used as target.
-By authors. The measured cumulative yields were
converted into chain yields assuming unchanged charge
division and the yield-mass distribution was
obtained at all energies.
Irradiated targets were transported using
helium-jet facility.  The transit time was measured to
be around 300 milliseconds.
The maximum error in the yields.
The maximum error in the half-life
measurements.
Tbl.2 from Zeitschr.Phys.,A345(1993)401
Correction
Last checking has been done.
SD: Corrections in Subents 004,013.
ERR-ANALYS updated.
.The chain yield of the isobaric chain.
.The chain yield of the isobaric chain.
The determination of absolute yields was not possible.
-By authors. The 97.5-KeV gamma-line assigned to Nb-99
has shown a decay behavior consistent with the
reported half-life (15-sec) only at 28.5 and 39.7-MeV.
But at higher energies, possible due to significant
contribution from isomers, the 97.5-KeV gamma-line
showed a much longer half-life.
SD: SF6=SIG -> FY in REACTION code.
-By authors.  The gamma intensity values are unknown
and was computed so that the chain yields fit into
earlier mass distribution data for the same  fissioning
system at 28.5-MeV.  See  REL-REF.  The same gamma
Intensities values were then used to compute the yields
of these nuclei at the other three incident energies.
.The chain yield of the isobaric chain.

*By authors*.The 275-KeV gamma-line has been assigned
to two nuclei, Rh-111 and Nb-101. This is because the
decay curves at all the four different energies were
not good enough to extract the individual contributions
of the two isotopes.
